title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 407.1110 - Advisory group for consumer protection from telemarketers — publication of information on consumer rights
text:
407.1110.  Advisory group for consumer protection from telemarketers — publication of information on consumer rights. — The attorney general shall establish an advisory group composed of government entities, local telecommunications companies, businesses, and senior citizen and other community advocates to compile and promote a list of educational literature to help consumers understand their options with regard to telephone solicitations.  The attorney general shall work with local exchange telecommunications companies to disseminate to their residential subscribers information about the availability of and instructions about how to request educational literature from the attorney general.  The attorney general may enter into agreements with those companies for the purpose of dissemination of the educational literature.  The attorney general shall include on his or her internet website information that informs residential subscribers of their rights to be placed on a no-call list and the various methods, including notice to the attorney general, of placing their names on this no-call list.  The attorney general shall have this literature developed for dissemination to the public no later than January 1, 2001.
(L. 2000 S.B. 763 § 407.1113)
